key_departures.impact: Released by the Cardinals at the start of the new league year after playing just 5 games in 2025 due to injury. Was the franchise QB since 2019 (No. 1 overall pick). Arizona carries significant dead cap (~$54.7M pre-June 1, or spread over two years post-June 1). The QB room is now Brissett + Minshew — a clear bridge setup with no franchise-caliber replacement in hand.

Signed a 3yr/$33M deal (max $36M) with Dallas. Thompson was one of Arizona's defensive cornerstones, starting 87 of 99 games since 2021 alongside Budda Baker. His departure breaks up the Baker-Thompson safety tandem. Dadrion Taylor-Demerson projects as the replacement, but is unproven as a full-time starter.
Cut for ~$9.4M in cap savings. Tomlinson accounted for 555 snaps in 2025. His release leaves a meaningful hole in the interior defensive line that Arizona has addressed partially via free agency.
Released alongside Davis-Gaither for combined cap savings of ~$10M+. Rotational IDL depth lost.
Cut for cap savings. Rotational linebacker depth reduced, though the Cardinals retain Cody Simon and Mack Wilson as their projected starting duo.
Hit free agency as a UFA after a disappointing 2-year, $30M deal that saw him miss significant time due to injuries and underperform when healthy. Right tackle is now an acute need.
Veteran swing tackle departed as a UFA. Beachum provided depth and experienced spot-starting. Loss compounds the right tackle need.
Veteran pass rusher hit free agency ahead of his age-40 season. Provided leadership and depth at IDL/EDGE. Expected departure given age; some snap contribution will need to be replaced.
Slot receiver experiment did not pan out. His departure creates a vacancy in the WR room that Arizona addressed with the Bourne signing.
Versatile slot/ST option departed. Cardinals lost WR slot depth but partially addressed with Bourne signing.

signings.analysis: The top-ranked guard in free agency per The Athletic's top-150 list, Seumalo is a Pro Bowl veteran (2024) who brings immediate starting-caliber play to a Cardinals IOL room that ranked among the worst in the NFL. His 3yr/$31.5M deal is starter-tier at guard (AAV $10.5M sits squarely in the $10-16M starter band). He projects to start immediately. However, Seumalo is 32 and dealt with two injuries in 2025, limiting him to 14 games. The team still needs a right guard or left guard partner, and center (Froholdt) remains a depth-level player. This partially addresses IOL — the acute guard need is reduced but line depth and OT remain critical. Need drops from tier 1 to tier 2.

One-year deal at minimum-adjacent money for a versatile lineman who has played all five OL spots except center. Pryor started just 40 of 109 career games and played only 12% of offensive snaps for the Eagles in 2025. He is a band-aid on a gaping OT wound — with both Jonah Williams and Kelvin Beachum gone, Arizona has no legitimate starting right tackle. The team officially acknowledges they're still searching. OT remains a tier-1 draft priority despite Pryor's addition. His value is depth and versatility, not a solution.

Solid depth signing at mid-tier RB money ($6.1M AAV). Allgeier never fumbled in four NFL seasons and brings red-zone and short-yardage physicality. He joins a room with James Conner (restructured) and Trey Benson. RB was not a critical pre-FA need; this adds quality and addresses roster depth behind an aging Conner. Arizona's backfield is now actually loaded (Conner + Allgeier + Benson). RB is no longer any kind of need. No-need position signing.

Two-year deal at $5M AAV ($10M base, max $12M with incentives, $7.5M guaranteed per OTC). Bourne fills the slot/WR3 vacancy left by Zay Jones and Greg Dortch. He had 37 catches for 551 yards in 2025 with the 49ers and brings familiarity with HC Mike LaFleur's scheme from their shared time in San Francisco. The guaranteed structure ($7.5M over 2 years) signals a real investment, not a camp body. However, WR behind Marvin Harrison Jr. and Michael Wilson remains thin — this addresses the immediate slot need but Arizona could still target a WR in the mid-rounds of the draft for long-term depth. WR need moves from tier 2 to tier 3.

One-year deal worth $5.75M base (up to $8M with incentives). Minshew is on his sixth team in seven years and joins Jacoby Brissett (retained under contract) as the QB room post-Kyler Murray. This is explicitly a bridge/depth signing, not a franchise QB solution. The Cardinals missed out on Malik Willis (signed with Miami on 3yr/$67.5M) and have not addressed the starter problem. Brissett is the de facto QB1 for 2026 but could be traded. QB remains the single most pressing need heading into the 2026 draft — Arizona holds the No. 3 overall pick. This signing does not resolve the need in any meaningful way.

Two-year, $11.5M deal with $7M guaranteed — a meaningful commitment that signals Lopez as a real roster piece, not a camp signing. Lopez (6-2, 312 lbs) played all 17 games for Detroit in 2025, recording 30 tackles and 2 sacks on 425 snaps (39% of defensive snaps). He returns to Arizona having previously played here in 2023-24 under DC Nick Rallis, providing scheme continuity. He replaces Dalvin Tomlinson's snap volume and profiles as a rotational nose tackle. Combined with the re-signing of LJ Collier, the IDL depth is patched — but no elite pass rusher or true three-down DT was acquired. IDL need moves from tier 1 (post-Tomlinson/Nichols cuts) to tier 2.

Fourth consecutive one-year deal with Arizona. Collier is a health risk — two of his three seasons in Arizona have been injury-shortened, including a knee injury that limited him to 4 games in 2025. When healthy (2024), he contributed 3.5 sacks and 29 tackles across 15 games. Low-cost depth re-sign that adds experience but carries health uncertainty. Does not move the needle on IDL need.

Conner's contract was restructured (not released as many expected) to keep him at a reduced cap number. He returns as the RB1 when healthy, though injuries (season-ending ankle in Week 3, 2025) remain a concern entering his age-30 season. The restructure, combined with Allgeier signing, gives Arizona a solid 1-2 RB punch. Re-signing keeps a position of relative strength intact.

Contract restructured to save ~$7.2M. Arizona opted to retain his experience in a young CB room rather than release him. He provides veteran depth alongside Will Johnson and Denzel Burke. CB was not a critical pre-FA need given the young depth on the roster, and this move maintains that status quo. No meaningful need shift.

Re-signed on a one-year deal. Ryland was not tendered as a restricted free agent after hitting 75.8% of FGs (25/33) in 2025, a dip from 87.5% in 2024. He returns but will compete with Joshua Karty. Kicker position stable.

Re-signed on a one-year deal. When healthy, Gillikin averaged a career-high 51.7 yards per punt in 2025 (5 games). Injury history is a concern (missed 12 games in 2025). Quality punter retained at low cost.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Kyler Murray officially released. Malik Willis lost to Miami (3yr/$67.5M). Room is now Brissett (bridge) + Minshew (1yr/$5.75M backup). Both are stopgap options — neither is a franchise QB. Arizona holds the No. 3 overall pick and this is the defining question of their entire offseason. QB is a tier-1 draft need, potentially the top pick used here.

Both Jonah Williams and Kelvin Beachum departed in free agency, leaving zero proven starting right tackles on roster. Matt Pryor (1yr, depth) is explicitly not the answer — the team and beat reporters acknowledge they're still looking. Paris Johnson Jr. holds LT; right tackle is vacant. Remains a tier-1 need — likely top-3 draft priority if not QB. Could target in rounds 1-3.

Isaac Seumalo signing (3yr/$31.5M, Pro Bowl guard) directly addresses the interior OL need at guard. He is a legitimate starter who will upgrade the unit immediately. However, the rest of the interior remains in flux — Evan Brown and Isaiah Adams compete at the other guard spot, and center (Froholdt) is unproven. IOL drops from critical to moderate — still draft-worthy in rounds 3-5.

Lost Tomlinson (cut), Nichols (cut), and Calais Campbell (UFA). Signed Roy Lopez (2yr/$11.5M, $7M gtd) and re-signed LJ Collier (1yr/$2.5M). Lopez is a solid rotational nose tackle who brings familiar scheme experience under DC Nick Rallis. Collier provides injury-dependent depth. The IDL unit has bodies but no elite pass rusher or three-down anchor. Remains a round 3-5 draft target.

Jalen Thompson departed for the Cowboys on 3yr/$33M — a significant loss after seven seasons as a Cardinals starter. Dadrion Taylor-Demerson is the presumed replacement alongside Budda Baker, but is unproven as a full-time starter. No S was signed in free agency to address this. Safety escalates to a tier-1 need — likely rounds 2-3 in the draft.

Cardinals lost Zay Jones and Greg Dortch (UFAs). Signed Kendrick Bourne (2yr/$10M, $7.5M gtd) as a WR3/slot complement to Marvin Harrison Jr. and Michael Wilson. Bourne is a familiar fit in LaFleur's scheme and addresses the slot vacancy. However, WR depth beyond the top three is still thin. Need drops to tier 3 — a mid-round WR for development/depth is still desirable but not urgent.

No EDGE additions in free agency. Josh Sweat had a career year in sacks in 2025 but Arizona still needs a suitable running mate per the team's own admission. No move was made here. EDGE unchanged at tier 2 — a rounds 2-4 draft target.

Akeem Davis-Gaither released (rotational). Cody Simon and Mack Wilson projected as starters. No LB signing in FA. Depth remains thin but starting duo is intact. LB unchanged at tier 3 — depth add in rounds 4-7 is fine.

key_additions.headline: Top-ranked free agent guard (Pro Bowl 2024) immediately upgrades a historically weak Cardinals interior OL
Familiar scheme fit returns to Arizona to replace Tomlinson's snaps after posting 2 sacks and 30 tackles for Detroit in 2025
LaFleur coaching reunion fills slot/WR3 vacancy left by Jones and Dortch departures
Physical, never-fumbled goal-line back adds insurance and depth behind the injury-prone James Conner
Bridge backup added alongside Brissett as Cardinals punt on 2026 QB market ahead of No. 3 overall pick
Veteran swing tackle provides stop-gap depth at right tackle while Cardinals seek a real starter via the draft

key_losses.headline: Franchise QB era ends after injury-plagued 2025 (5 games played); Cardinals absorbed massive dead cap to move on
7-year Cardinals starter and Budda Baker's long-time partner in the secondary departs for Dallas, creating a genuine S need
Starting DT (555 snaps in 2025) cut for cap relief, accelerating Arizona's need to restock the interior defensive line
Injury-plagued right tackle departs, leaving Arizona without a proven starter at the position heading into the draft
Veteran pass rusher and locker room leader departs as a UFA, further thinning the Cardinals' defensive front
